title: Otitis externa follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.
examination.rationale: The exact acute-otitis-externa section supports interval pain, itch, discharge, hearing, functional impact, associated features, relevant negatives, modifying factors, vital signs, general appearance, and ENT examination without asserting persistence or complication.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

source.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements

exact_section.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors

referral.text: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.
referral.rationale: The exact key-action section supports clinician concern, reviewed specialist information, entered plan, safety net, and arranged follow-up without generating eardrops, antibiotics, wick placement, culture, procedure, or referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

source.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements

source_limitations: Laterality, symptom course, discharge, hearing, canal and membrane findings, diabetes, immune status, medicine, adherence, adverse effects, culture, diagnosis, treatment, complication, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
